description: Guard moves to back_room (clean), then attempts a direct back_room→entrance jump — B1 only, no A1.
adversarial_note: Uses the same character as tc-b01 (guard) but from the opposite extremity: guard is at back_room and attempts to reach entrance directly. The non-adjacent pair is back_room→entrance — the reverse direction of tc-b01's entrance→back_room. Together, tc-b01 and tc-b04 cover both directions of the same non-adjacent pair with the same character, confirming that B1 is bidirectional and not an artifact of the direction in which the constraint was written. This is not a duplicate of tc-b01 — the setup positions differ, the non-adjacent pairs differ, and the spatial context differs.

condition_notes.A: ABox never updates. Oracle sees guard at main_hall (baseline). main_hall→entrance is adjacent — B1 does not fire. Zero oracle violations expected under Condition A.
condition_notes.B: Turn 1 commits guard→back_room. Turn 2: back_room→entrance fires B1.

turns.prompt: The guard steps into the back room to retrieve his cloak.
The guard, cloak in hand, strides directly from the back room to the entrance without passing through the main hall.
